changes.reason: Config-only dormant concept test: add exactly one pin-avoid profile to the VALID_0DTE router while preserving the current active profile order and all existing 0DTE engines. This tests attribution without touching no-0DTE, 1DTE, exits, or global veto thresholds.

Enable the dormant charm-wall pin-avoid concept so the router can expose whether existing C# playbook logic can isolate charm/pin tail states from profitable charm_pin_pullback_long and charm_wall_breakaway_long trades.

recommendation.expected_effect: This should show whether the dormant pin-avoid lane is reachable and whether it captures high-charm-wall or pin-risk entries that currently land in charm_pin_pullback_long, the profile owning the -513.4 MAE tail. Success is not just more trades: combined charm-pin/charm-wall/pin-avoid PnL should remain near leader levels, worst MAE should improve or stay flat, slow_12h must remain zero, and protected fast extended-VWAP winners should not be materially cannibalized. Main tradeoff is router cannibalization of clean charm-pin or charm-wall winners.
recommendation.label: promote
recommendation.rationale: The current leader base should not repeat run_034 no-0DTE tightening, squeeze exit compression, direct 1DTE tightening, or broad global VWAP/ADX pruning. The strongest next experiment is a single-family strategy-profile discovery split: charm_pin_pullback_long contributes $1261.5 over 34 trades but owns the global worst MAE at -513.4, while protected extended-VWAP winners overlap the same static features. A dormant pin-avoid router split tests whether existing playbook logic can separate that tail without bluntly deleting profitable extension trades.

strategy_hypotheses.evidence: charm_pin_pullback_long is profitable but tail-heavy: 34 VALID_0DTE trades, $1261.5 PnL, median hold 5.0 minutes, but worst MAE -513.4. charm_wall_breakaway_long adds convex upside with 5 trades and $985.6 PnL, so broad pruning around charm/wall extension is dangerous. A dormant pin-avoid profile can provide attribution without changing existing exits or global vetoes.
strategy_hypotheses.name: Dormant Charm Wall Pin-Avoid Router Split
strategy_hypotheses.risk: The profile may be unreachable, or router ordering may cannibalize clean charm-pin/charm-wall winners instead of isolating tail entries.
strategy_hypotheses.trigger: VALID_0DTE high charm-wall or pin-risk state that currently routes into charm_pin_pullback_long or charm_wall_breakaway_long.
strategy_hypotheses.validation_metric: Pin-avoid profile is reachable with positive or risk-reducing attribution; combined charm-pin/charm-wall/pin-avoid net PnL retains at least 98% of run_029, total score is flat or better, worst MAE improves or stays no worse, slow_12h remains 0, and protected fast extended-VWAP winner retention is at least 95%.

strategy_hypotheses.evidence: The worst charm-pin tail took about 80 minutes to reach TP and had delayed early progress, while protected fast extended-VWAP winners resolve quickly with median MAE around -14.9. Static config thresholds overlap both populations.
strategy_hypotheses.name: Charm Pin Early-Progress Tail Classifier
strategy_hypotheses.risk: Path-aware logic can overfit a small tail sample or reject clean high-extension winners unless protected-winner retention is explicitly validated.
strategy_hypotheses.trigger: charm_pin_pullback_long entries with high ES-VWAP extension or recent-high rejection that fail to make early positive progress.
strategy_hypotheses.validation_metric: Reduce charm_pin_pullback_long worst MAE toward <= -403 while retaining >=90% charm-pin PnL, >=95% protected fast extended-VWAP winners, total net profit >=98% of leader, and slow_12h remains 0.
